explicit_text: I was born at St. Anthony’s in Oklahoma City, and my family has deep roots there. My mother, Katie, grew up in Oklahoma City and attended Capitol Hill High School. My grandfather and grandmother lived in Oklahoma City until they passed away, so the city has always been a significant part of my family history.

explicit_text: My grandfather was named Lon Chaney, but not the famous actor Lon Chaney. He was part of my family lineage, and I always felt a connection to him through my mother’s side. Though he wasn’t the Hollywood star, he was an important figure in my family’s story.

explicit_text: My father was from Blanchard, Oklahoma, and he served two hitches in the war before meeting my mother. After they married and I was born, we moved around a bit, but Blanchard was where my dad originally came from. It’s a small town that shaped his early life before the war.

explicit_text: My dad started a propane business out in Coweta County after working with his uncle. While we lived in Binger, Oklahoma, he was busy running that business and delivering propane to irrigation wells and homes. Coweta County was important to our family’s livelihood and my dad’s work ethic.

explicit_text: I am part Choctaw, about one-sixteenth, though we never got officially on the rolls. My grandfather Clay Bench’s mother was a full-blood Choctaw, but the family was fostered out and didn’t register. Still, I’ve always been proud of my Choctaw heritage and it’s a meaningful part of who I am.

explicit_text: When I was three years old, we moved to Binger, Oklahoma, which is about fifty-five to sixty miles southwest of Oklahoma City and west of Norman. explicit_text: The Chicago Cubs were very interested in me when I was coming up as a player.
